rationale: The citing paper is a conceptual editorial on how to write “sticky” JMR articles and says it illustrates points with examples from papers in the June 2008 issue, whereas the cited paper is an empirical brand-extension/self-construal study in that same issue. With only a bare DOI match and no citation-context evidence of methodological or theoretical dependence, this looks like an in-issue illustrative mention rather than substantive use.
